# LedgerLens Eval Datasets

This directory contains versioned eval datasets for LedgerLens categorization.

## Status

**v0 dataset is first-pass.** Labels were generated by `claude-code-session-04` and are pending human review by the project owner. Do not treat label accuracy as ground truth until the user has reviewed and revised. The decision to start from LLM-proposed labels — and the review process that closes that gap — is recorded in [ADR-0003](../../docs/adr/0003-synthetic-eval-data.md).

ARCHITECTURE.md §7 calls for a held-out slice of ~100 transactions across the three businesses, never used during prompt development. The v0 dataset does not yet partition into dev vs. held-out; the entire dataset is currently dev. The held-out split will be drawn from a future `v1` (or a sibling `v0-held-out` directory) once the categorization pipeline is far enough along to risk overfitting.

## Structure

```
evals/datasets/
├── README.md
└── v0/
    ├── index.json            # dataset metadata, business list, counts
    ├── coffee-shop/
    │   ├── business.json
    │   ├── chart_of_accounts.json
    │   └── transactions.json
    ├── design-agency/
    │   └── (same three files)
    └── auto-repair/
        └── (same three files)
```

## Schemas

### `index.json`

```jsonc
{
  "version": "v0",
  "created": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "labeler": "claude-code-session-04, pending human review",
  "businesses": [
    { "id": "...", "name": "...", "industry": "...",
      "transaction_count": 100, "adversarial_count": 10 }
  ],
  "total_transactions": 300,
  "total_adversarial": 30,
  "notes": "..."
}
```

### `business.json`

```jsonc
{
  "id": "coffee-shop",
  "name": "Lighthouse Roasters",
  "industry": "Specialty coffee retail",
  "description": "...",
  "fiscal_year_start": "01-01",
  "typical_monthly_revenue_usd": 35000,
  "notes": "Design intent for the labeler — not consumed by the categorizer."
}
```

### `chart_of_accounts.json` — list of accounts

```jsonc
{
  "code": "6010",
  "name": "Cost of Goods Sold - Green Coffee",
  "description": "...",   // the categorizer reads this at retrieval time
  "parent_code": null,
  "type": "expense"        // asset | liability | equity | revenue | cogs | expense | other_income | other_expense
}
```

Account codes follow standard bookkeeping convention: 1xxx assets, 2xxx liabilities, 3xxx equity, 4xxx revenue, 5xxx COGS, 6xxx expenses, 7xxx other income, 8xxx other expense.

### `transactions.json` — list of transactions

```jsonc
{
  "id": "coffee-shop-tx-001",
  "date": "2025-07-03",
  "amount_cents": -45200,                  // signed integer cents; outflows negative
  "raw_description": "ROYAL COFFEE IMPORTERS WHOLESALE",
  "proposed_category_code": "5010",        // must reference a code in this business's chart_of_accounts.json
  "label_confidence": "high",              // high | medium | low
  "is_adversarial": false,
  "reasoning": "1-2 sentences explaining the categorization.",
  "labeler_notes": null                     // populated when the labeler wants to flag the entry for review
}
```

## Labeling conventions

- `label_confidence`:
  - `high` — unambiguous; no reasonable labeler would disagree (~70%).
  - `medium` — defensible; a different labeler might choose differently (~20%).
  - `low` — genuinely ambiguous; part of the adversarial slice (~10%).
- `is_adversarial: true` marks transactions to be scored separately in the eval harness, per ARCHITECTURE §7. Currently 1:1 with `label_confidence == "low"`.
- `amount_cents` is signed integer cents. Outflows (expenses, purchases) negative; inflows (revenue, refunds) positive.
- `proposed_category_code` is verified by a referential-integrity check at commit time and at eval-load time.
- `labeler_notes` is `null` by default. Populated when the labeler wants to flag the entry — typically to ask the human reviewer to disambiguate, or to note a schema limitation (e.g., transactions that should be split across multiple categories but cannot be under the current schema).

## Versioning

Datasets are versioned by directory (`v0`, `v1`, ...). The eval harness pins to a version. Revisions create a new version directory; prior versions are preserved unchanged for historical comparison. Files within a version are never edited in place after the version is published.

## See also

- [ARCHITECTURE.md §7](../../docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) — eval methodology and gating rules.
- [ADR-0003](../../docs/adr/0003-synthetic-eval-data.md) — decision to use synthetic data.
